2605.22380.title: Multi-Stage Training for Abusive Comment Detection in Indic Languages
2605.22380.paper_summary: In recent years social media has become an increasingly popular tool for communication. People use it to share their ideas, exchange information, and discuss thoughts. Given its prevalence and widespread reach, social media must remain a safe space for people. Content generated on social media can be abusive and it has become increasingly important to detect such content. In this paper, we use a language-based preprocessing and an ensemble of several models and analyze their performance of abusive comment detection. Through extensive experimentation, we propose a pipeline that minimizes the false-positive rate (marking non-abusive as abusive) so that these systems can detect abusive comments without undermining the freedom of expression.
2605.22380.paper_summary_zh: 近年來，社交媒體已成為一個越來越受歡迎的溝通工具。<lb/>人們使用它來分享想法、交換信息和討論觀點。<lb/>考慮到其普及性和廣泛的影響力，社交媒體必須保持為人們提供一個安全的空間。<lb/>在社交媒體上生成的內容可能具有攻擊性，因此檢測此類內容變得越來越重要。<lb/>在本文中，我們使用基於語言的預處理和多個模型的集成，並分析它們在攻擊性評論檢測中的表現。<lb/>通過廣泛的實驗，我們提出了一個管道，以最小化假陽性率（將非攻擊性標記為攻擊性），以便這些系統能夠檢測攻擊性評論而不損害言論自由。
2605.22380.author: Pranshu Rastogi et.al.
2605.22380.authors: Pranshu Rastogi, Madhav Mathur, Ramaneswaran S, Kshitij Mohan
